commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=adb584cd2ced7f1cd694ab29e95314e8c57cbf90)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a vendored Crabbox skill, `.crabbox.yaml`, a `workflow_dispatch` Crabbox hydrate workflow, actionlint runner-label config, and CODEOWNERS entries for those automation surfaces.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows the PR adds a vendored Crabbox skill with non-slacrawl `pnpm` recipes while current main is a Go repo and the predecessor was closed in favor of the shared skill source. There is no runtime app bug to reproduce.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to decide whether this repo intentionally vendors Crabbox despite the prior shared-source decision; automation should not choose that policy on its own.

target.title: chore: add constrained Crabbox setup
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the exact Crabbox skill copied from `openclaw/openclaw`.
- Adds constrained Crabbox config and hydrate workflow with repo-specific self-hosted runner labels.
- Adds actionlint runner-label config and CODEOWNERS coverage for the new automation surfaces.

This is the narrowed replacement shape for the earlier broad setup baseline. It intentionally does not add CodeQL, stale automation, licensing changes, Dependabot, package-manager files, or unrelated policy defaults.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- Ruby YAML parse for `.crabbox.yaml`, `.github/actionlint.yaml`, and `.github/workflows/crabbox-hydrate.yml`
- `actionlint -config-file .github/actionlint.yaml .github/workflows/crabbox-hydrate.yml`
- Crabbox skill SHA-256 matched `openclaw/openclaw`: `ed512c0b0385fae7f6c5c14a7e9e6236ab68936506687a99ca976873492bdc43`
- Private-path scan for new public files
- `test -z "$(gofmt -l .)"`

## Notes

No live Crabbox lease was started for this setup-only patch.
target.author: vincentkoc
